description: Root React component and entry-point page of the TabCommand UI, mounted at / via React 18 createRoot. Reads uxSettings / labels / activeTabs / allUrls through the Chrome abstraction and reacts to chrome.storage.onChanged. Renders the sidebar — AppBrand, Settings + ThemeToggle tools, Search, the SearchHint line, the Favorites section, the load-aware Triage card (Home only), and a footer holding the relocated Browser Load gauge plus the tab/group counts and the Import/Export link — alongside a content area that routes between the Home grid (Labels + Tabs under a DragDropContext, persisted via the applyDrag reducer), Load, History, ImportExport, and UrlDetails pages.
name: App

description: Sidebar wordmark: the 4-color TabCommand mark plus a Tab/Command text wordmark in their brand colors. Clicking it returns to Home via the parent-supplied onClick.
name: AppBrand

description: Callback-based storage abstraction faithfully ported from the reference TabCommand. Wraps chrome.storage.local with get(from, keys, callback) / set(from, updates) / remove(from, keys), preserving the leading `from` debug-label arg. On get it applies default-hydration (labels/uxSettings/autoClosed -> {}, activeTabs/allUrls/previousLabels -> []) and strips each previousLabels entry's timestamp while dropping falsy entries. The storage contract every feature plan's reads build on.
name: Chrome

description: Renders a url row's favicon with a graceful fallback: the site's image when available, otherwise a deterministic colored monogram tile (via faviconMonogram). Falls back on image load error too.
name: Favicon

description: One favorite on the Favorites View All page: favicon, title, a stats strip that explains the rank (visit count in the window, last-visited via relativeTime, the decay score), and a UsageSparkline of usage over time. A site open in a tab gets the accent open cue; a hidden (removed) favorite is dimmed and shows a Bring back button instead of being filtered out. The whole row opens or focuses the tab on click; onOpen and onBringBack are supplied by the page.
name: FavoriteRow

description: Sidebar section listing the user's genuinely most-visited sites, up to 10 that clear the minimum-visit threshold, ordered by the frequency times recency-decay blend in the pure rankFavorites util so the list reads as favorites over the recent past rather than all-time. Reads allUrls plus the matching url-* records from chrome.storage.local, and also reads activeTabs and favoritesHidden. Builds an excludedKeys set, sites open in a Chrome-pinned tab and user-removed sites are dropped entirely, AND an openKeys set of non-pinned active-tab urlKeys passed to rankFavorites so a site currently open in a non-pinned tab has its in-progress visit discounted rather than excluded. A favorite that rankFavorites flags isOpen, currently open in a non-pinned tab, renders with the Favorites-item--open modifier giving a subtle lime accent-tinted background and left stripe as an already-open cue. Stays live via chrome.storage.onChanged on allUrls / url-* / activeTabs / favoritesHidden. Renders a Favicon plus title per row plus a hover-revealed remove control that adds the site's urlKey to favoritesHidden, hiding it from Favorites while keeping it in Search/History. On row click focuses an already-open tab or opens a new one. Renders nothing when there are no qualifying favorites.
name: Favorites

description: The Reset favorites tracking control on the Favorites View All page. A quiet utility button at rest; clicking it reveals an inline two-step confirm with a destructive Yes reset everything and a Cancel, so the destructive action takes a deliberate second click. Owns only the confirm/cancel toggle state; the reset itself is onReset, supplied by the page.
name: FavoritesResetControl

description: History page component: lists every closed/visited tab grouped by Today / Yesterday / Earlier this week, each row colored by its group label and offering a Reopen action.
name: History

description: One closed/visited tab row in the History list: a group-color dot, the tab favicon (monogram fallback via Favicon), the title with a full title+URL tooltip, a mono timestamp when known, and a Reopen action that calls back with the urlKey.
name: HistoryRow

description: Local Lucide-style inline-SVG icon set (1.9px stroke, currentColor, round joins) that replaces @ant-design/icons. A name->path lookup returning static SVG; color and size are driven by the caller.
name: Icon

description: The Import / Export page (entry point for Pages.IMPORTEXPORT in App): a recover/backup view reached from the sidebar Import/Export link. Reads labels + previousLabels from Chrome storage and renders the Current export (groups serialized to JSON, sorted and url-resolved) plus a read-only Previous textarea per snapshot; the Import textarea + button parse pasted JSON to restore groups, swallowing malformed JSON, then call onComplete to return Home. Pure logic lives in utils/importExport; also covered by the import-export-backup application scenario.
name: ImportExport

description: Global keydown pub/sub over a single document.onkeydown listener: add/remove subscribers and trigger fans an event (real or synthetic) to all of them. Powers Search's Cmd/Ctrl+F focus + Escape close and SearchResults' arrow/enter navigation.
name: KeyDown

description: One group card: a colored title bar (click selects the group via uxSettings.selectedLabel), a menu (edit LabelForm + share + delete), and a full-card Droppable spanning the title bar and a vertical list of Url rows split into active above inactive. While a tab is dragged the whole card highlights in the group color when it is the cursor target; supports removeUrl and is itself draggable for reordering.
name: LabelCollection

description: Create/edit form for a label (group): a title input and a swatch picker from Colors. On submit writes the label into the labels map keyed by title — deriving backgroundColor (Colors[title.length % len]) and position when absent, and deleting the old key on a rename.
name: LabelForm

description: The collapsible "+ Add Group" affordance at the top of the Labels grid. Toggles an expanded wrapper (with a click-catching #BackgroundOverlay) that hosts a LabelForm for creating a new group.
name: LabelFormContainer

description: A small uppercase section header used inside a group card to demarcate a subset of Url rows. Renders a lime live-status dot, the section label, and a compact count pushed to the right. Purely presentational; the parent decides when to render it.
name: LabelSectionHeader

description: The groups area filling the Home center: reads the labels map + uxSettings from chrome.storage, sorts groups by title then position, pins the selected label expanded above the grid, and chunks the rest into responsive horizontal Droppable rows of LabelCollection. Shows the Add Group CTA when empty.
name: Labels

untestabilityReason.justification: Layout-only stub — renders an empty div with id, no props or logic.

description: The Load page (entry point for the LOAD page in App): composes LoadPerTabNote (shown when per-tab data is unavailable), a list of LoadUrl cards built from activeTabs + the per-URL storage objects, and the raw process table (LoadProcesses), plus a Home link that writes uxSettings.page back to Home. The sidebar LoadMeter gauge links here.
name: Load

description: Sidebar gauge: two concentric SVG arcs (cpu inner, memory outer) whose filled fraction reflects the processTotals storage key, rendered via gradient-path per-segment fill mutation. Reads processTotals on mount and via chrome.storage.onChanged; pure logic is factored into deriveGaugeTotals + gaugeFillPercent. Composes LoadMeterCaption for the source-aware label beneath it. GradientPath calls are guarded to no-op under jsdom; the visual is verified by scenario capture. Clicking it navigates to the Load page.
name: LoadMeter

untestabilityReason.justification: Layout-only stub — renders an empty div, no props or logic.

description: Source-aware label beneath the LoadMeter gauge. Reads the loadDataSource storage marker: system -> a subtle whole-browser-load caption; none -> an explicit No load data indicator (so the empty gauge reads as legible, not broken); processes/unknown -> nothing. Owns its own storage read so the gauge stays purely about the arcs.
name: LoadMeterCaption

description: Honest note on the Load page shown when per-tab CPU/memory is unavailable. Reads loadDataSource: on system/none it explains that per-tab/per-process data needs Chrome Dev channel (chrome.processes) so the empty per-tab bars and process table read as intentional; on processes (or before the marker is known) it renders nothing. Owns its own storage read, sibling of LoadUrl/LoadProcesses.
name: LoadPerTabNote

description: The Load page's raw per-process table: one block per OS process showing task titles plus CPU and private-memory readouts with inline load bars, formatted via humanReadableNumber. Driven by chrome.processes.onUpdatedWithMemory — the live API in a packaged extension; in the codeyam preview the chrome shim emits any seeded `processes` snapshot so the table can be demonstrated (empty when nothing is seeded).
name: LoadProcesses

description: A single card in the Load page's URL list: the tab's favicon (default fallback) and title linked out in a new tab, plus a per-tab load readout (average CPU + private memory with a severity-colored bar via summarizeProcessLoad) when the URL's sampled processes data is present.
name: LoadUrl

description: Sidebar search box: builds a minisearch index over labels and their labeled URLs (fields labelTitle/urlTitle/url/notes with boosts), rebuilds it on chrome.storage onChanged (labels change, or a labeled URL notes change), and renders #Search-Input. Typing runs a prefix search; results are deduped + segmented (segmentSearchResults) into the SearchResults overlay (labels, urls capped at 10). Cmd/Ctrl+F focuses, Escape/#BackgroundOverlay closes. A build-token guards overlapping async rebuilds. Index document shaping is factored into buildSearchDocuments/buildUrlDocuments.
name: Search

untestabilityReason.justification: Layout-only stub — renders an empty div, no props or logic.

description: Static single-line muted hint rendered under the search bar on Home: tells the user they can just start typing to search. No props, no branching, single visual state.
name: SearchHint

untestabilityReason.justification: presentation-only static text, no props or branching, single visual state

description: The search overlay rendered by Search: sections for Groups (label hits, colored icon), Grouped URLs (favicon + title, with a highlighted notes snippet via searchNotesSnippet when the match is in notes), and an Archived URLs affordance, plus a No Results empty state. Keyboard navigation (Arrow up/down + Enter via the KeyDown pub/sub) and mouse hover move the selection; activating a label selects it and routes Home, activating a url focuses/opens its tab, and the edit pencil routes to the URL detail page.
name: SearchResults

description: Sidebar gear popover of preferences. Always-visible controls: a Theme Day/Night/System segmented control writing the themePreference storage key [read by useTheme], an Auto-close-after slider, and a Group-columns 2/3/4 segmented control persisted to the settings storage key. The Warn at and Heavy tab >= load sliders render ONLY when per-tab data exists [loadDataSource === processes]; on stable Chrome they hide. The gear sits at the top of the sidebar header, so the panel opens DOWNWARD and is centered on the gear, clamped into the viewport; it is rendered through a portal to document.body so the header transform cannot become the containing block for its fixed positioning [which would break placement and let content paint over it]. Both segmented controls compose the shared SettingsSegment sub-component.
name: Settings

description: Reusable segmented button-group used inside the Settings popover. Presentational only: renders one button per option, marks the active one via aria-pressed, and calls onChange with the value on click. The full flag selects the full-width Theme variant whose segments stretch evenly; omitted it is the inline digit picker. Used by both the Theme Day/Night/System control and the Group-columns control.
name: SettingsSegment

description: Read-only textarea paired with a copy-to-clipboard button that shows a transient 'Copied' confirmation, used to display an import/export snapshot value.
name: SnapshotField

description: Home tab list: renders Active Tabs, Automatically Closed, and collapsible History sections from chrome.storage; splits tabs into label groups vs an ungrouped remainder; filters browser-pinned tabs and expired auto-closed entries. Each row is a draggable Url.
name: Tabs

description: Sidebar light/dark toggle button: shows the moon glyph in light mode (click switches to dark) and the sun glyph in dark mode (click switches to light). Theme and toggle handler are owned by the parent.
name: ThemeToggle

description: Sidebar load-triage card (Home only) whose color tracks overall load vs settings.warnAt: red 'Running hot' with a 'Review N heavy tabs' CTA, amber 'Getting busy', or green 'Comfortable'. Counts heavy tabs (per-tab load >= heavyThreshold) and toggles the shared review mode. Renders ONLY when per-tab data exists (loadDataSource === 'processes'); hidden on stable Chrome (system/none/unknown), and the red high state is suppressed when no culprit tab crosses the threshold (heavyCount === 0). Pure load->level math is in loadLevel.
name: Triage

description: A single tab/url row: shows favicon + title (derived from urlKey when untitled), a process-load indicator (level + bar width from averaged CPU/memory), and a hover action row (pin/keep-open, edit, delete, close) wired to chrome.tabs and Chrome storage.
name: Url

description: The URL Details page (entry point for Pages.URL in App): the full-screen form to edit one saved URL — title/url/favicon inputs, a notes textarea, and removable Groups chips for its label memberships. Reads the per-URL object + labels from Chrome storage; Save persists the four form fields (notes only when non-empty) and returns Home, Cancel returns Home. Composes UrlField and UrlLabel; pure logic lives in utils/urlDetails.
name: UrlDetails

description: A single labeled field in the UrlDetails edit form — an <input> (Title/Url/Favicon) or, with `multiline`, a <textarea> (Notes). Reproduces the onKeyDown stopPropagation guard that keeps the app's global Esc/Search key handlers from firing while typing. Class is derived from the field name (UrlDetails-form-<name>).
name: UrlField

description: One removable 'Groups' chip in the UrlDetails form: a close icon plus the label title, with the title passed as the button value so the page handler knows which group to remove. Clicking it asks the page to drop this URL from that group.
name: UrlLabel

description: Two inline bar charts side by side on a FavoriteRow: usage over the last SPARK_DAYS days (recent daily rhythm) and the last SPARK_WEEKS weeks (longer-term trend that varies in height even when daily usage is steady). Built from bucketVisitsByDay and bucketVisitsByWeek, no charting library. An optional common `max` prop lets the page scale every row's bars to one shared maximum so heights are comparable across rows; without it each chart self-scales to its own busiest bucket. Visited buckets are tinted the brand accent.
name: UsageSparkline

description: The Favorites View All page: a full-page, uncapped list of every qualifying favorite ranked by decayed score, each rendered as a FavoriteRow showing its title, its URL as a clickable open control, rank-explaining stats, and a usage sparkline. Reads allUrls, activeTabs, and favoritesHidden from storage in an effect and stays live via chrome.storage.onChanged, calling rankFavorites with an Infinity limit and hiddenKeys so removed favorites appear dimmed with a Bring back action. Includes a FavoritesResetControl that clears the visit signal (visits/visitCount) and favoritesHidden so the favorites list starts over, leaving History and Search intact. Mirrors the History page shell and focuses an already-open tab on click or opens a new one. Reached from the sidebar Favorites section View All link.
name: ViewAllFavorites

description: Pure ring-buffer helper for the grouping diagnostics trail: appends one breadcrumb to the current stored array and returns a new array trimmed to the most-recent cap entries, treating a missing or non-array store as empty and never mutating its input. Kept free of chrome/IO so it is unit-testable in isolation.
name: appendGroupingLog

description: Pure reducer behind App's onDragEnd: for URL drops moves a urlKey out of the source label and into the destination label at the drop index (reporting active tab ids to ungroup); for LABEL_COLLECTION drops reorders groups by rewriting each label's position. Returns null when there is no destination.
name: applyDrag

description: Maps a process's task tab ids back to tabs (chrome.tabs.get) and merges per-url process metrics into the updates object.
name: associateProcess

description: The Closer engine sweep: reads activeTabs/autoClosed/settings, prunes stale entries, then closes every eligible inactive tab via chrome.tabs.remove and records each into the autoClosed map so the Automatically Closed UI updates.
name: autoCloseSweep

description: Resolves the active inactivity threshold in minutes from settings.autoCloseMinutes, falling back to the default when unset and returning 0 when the user has set the Off position.
name: autoCloseThresholdMinutes

description: Navigates from the History page back to the Home page by updating uxSettings.page via Chrome storage.
name: back

description: Module-level navigation helper for the ViewAllFavorites page: resets uxSettings.page to Pages.HOME via the Chrome storage wrapper so the back button returns to the Home view. Mirrors the History page's back helper.
name: back

description: Buckets a closed/visited tab timestamp (ms epoch) into the History page day groups — Today, Yesterday, or Earlier this week — relative to a passed-in now. A missing timestamp falls into Earlier this week so the tab still appears. Exports HISTORY_BUCKETS for render order.
name: bucketByDay

description: Pure helper that buckets a site's visit timestamps into per-day counts over a trailing window of SPARK_DAYS days, oldest day first, relative to now. Visits older than the window or in the future are ignored. The Favorites View All page feeds the result to the UsageSparkline so a user sees a site's usage-over-time at a glance and understands why it ranks where it does.
name: bucketVisitsByDay

description: Pure helper that buckets a site's visit timestamps into per-week counts over a trailing window of SPARK_WEEKS weeks, oldest week first, relative to now. Each bucket spans 7 days and aggregates the visits within it, so the Favorites View All page's long-range sparkline varies in height even when daily usage is a steady one-per-day. Visits older than the window or in the future are ignored. Companion to bucketVisitsByDay for the short-range 7-day view.
name: bucketVisitsByWeek

description: Parses the pasted export JSON and builds the storage updates map: one per-URL object per url-<url> key plus the rebuilt labels map. Throws on malformed JSON so the page swallows it with a console.log, faithful to the reference saveImport.
name: buildImportUpdates

description: Turns the stored labels map into the synchronous half of the Search index: one minisearch document per label (id 'label-<title>' with color) plus a labelMap of every labeled urlKey to its label title. Pure, so index shaping is testable independent of minisearch/Chrome.
name: buildSearchDocuments

description: Builds one minisearch document per labeled URL from a labelMap and the per-URL storage records (urlTitle falling back to url; tagged with its label title). URLs with no backing record are skipped so a partially-seeded store never throws.
name: buildUrlDocuments

description: Builds the per-URL object persisted on Save from the UrlDetails form fields: always title/url/favicon, plus notes only when non-empty. Faithfully reproduces the reference's behavior of DROPPING the per-URL processes object (it writes only the form fields). Extracted from the page's handleSubmit.
name: buildUrlInfo

description: Clamps a number to the [0, 1] range, used to normalize CPU/memory ratios before rendering the load gauge.
name: clamp01

description: Moves a closed tab's url key to the front of `allUrls`, persists the reordered list, and invokes an optional callback.
name: closeUrl

description: Returns the de-duplicated list of urlKeys across the sorted labels — the exact set the Import/Export page hands to Chrome.get to resolve each per-URL object before serializing the export.
name: collectUrlKeys

description: Maps a viewport width in pixels to the most group columns the center pane can comfortably fit, using the widest-first COLUMN_BREAKPOINTS, returning 1 below the narrowest breakpoint. Used by effectiveColumns to cap the user's chosen count responsively.
name: columnsForWidth

description: Internal helper of deriveSystemTotals: average per-core busy fraction = delta(kernel+user)/delta(total) across processors between two chrome.system.cpu samples. Returns 0 (never NaN) for missing/empty/single-sample input and for a non-advancing counter.
name: cpuUtilization

description: Builds the chrome shim object backed by an in-memory store hydrated from window.localStorage (codeyam's seed inlet) over the shared KNOWN_KEYS. Serves storage.local get/set/remove/clear with localStorage mirroring (JSON string boundary kept inside the shim) and onChanged dispatch (areaName 'local'), plus callable no-op tabs/tabGroups/processes/runtime action stubs so interactive preview scenarios never throw on missing chrome APIs.
name: createChromeShim

description: Durable, runtime-toggleable diagnostic recorder for the tab-grouping decision points. Optionally emits a TC-GROUP console line when the compile-time DEBUG_GROUPING is on, and persists each breadcrumb to a capped groupingLog ring buffer in chrome.storage.local when the runtime debugGrouping flag is set, so grouping decisions survive MV3 service-worker restarts and are inspectable after the fact. Fire-and-forget: the storage round-trip never blocks the caller.
name: debugGroup

description: Pure helper that scores a site as the sum of its visits' exponentially-decayed weights, each 2 to the power of negative age over halfLifeMs, so a fresh visit is worth 1.0 and a visit one half-life old is worth 0.5. A future-dated timestamp from clock skew is clamped to weight 1. Returns 0 for empty or non-array input. This is the core ranking signal for Favorites: recent visits count more than old ones, and a site clears qualification when its summed decayed score reaches QUALIFY_MIN.
name: decayedVisitScore

description: Internal shim helper: invokes a callback asynchronously on a microtask (Promise.resolve().then) with an optional value, matching Chrome's async callback contract so consumers never depend on synchronous delivery. No-ops when no callback is supplied.
name: defer

description: Maps a stored processTotals record onto the two values the LoadMeter gauge renders: cpu (inner arc) and memory (outer arc, the sum of privateMemory + jsMemoryUsed bytes). Every field is optional so a partial or empty record reads as zero, never NaN. Factored out of LoadMeter's storage handler so the data binding is unit-testable independent of the SVG.
name: deriveGaugeTotals

description: Pure stable-Chrome fallback math for the LoadMeter gauge: turns two chrome.system.cpu samples plus a chrome.system.memory reading into a processTotals-shaped record normalized to the gauge's 0->max scale (cpu = avg per-core busy fraction * max.cpu; privateMemory = base + memory-pressure ratio * max.memory; base cancels so gauge fill == utilization). All fields guarded so empty/single-sample inputs read as zero, never NaN. Mirrors deriveGaugeTotals/gaugeFillPercent; fed by the service worker's pollSystemLoad.
name: deriveSystemTotals

description: Derives the UrlDetails 'Groups' chip titles from the labels map + a urlKey: the title of every label whose urlKeys contains that urlKey. The chips are derived state, never stored. Extracted from the page's setPartialState so it can be unit-tested in isolation.
name: deriveUrlLabels

description: Resolves the group droppableId under a viewport point via elementFromPoint, so a dragged tab targets whichever group the cursor is over instead of @hello-pangea/dnd's center-based collision. Returns null when the point is over no group card.
name: dropTargetIdAtPoint

description: Walks up from a DOM element to its enclosing group-card drop zone and returns that zone's @hello-pangea/dnd droppableId, or null when the element is not inside a group card. Backs cursor-based drop targeting.
name: dropTargetIdFromElement

description: Computes the column count the groups grid should render: the user's configured count from settings, capped by what the current viewport width can comfortably fit, floored at 1. Falls back to ColumnsDefault for missing or invalid input. Drives the chunk size and the --label-columns card-width variable in Labels.
name: effectiveColumns

description: Pulls a Chrome-auto-inherited tab back out of the group it was born into rather than making its URL sticky. Defers while the URL is unloaded, re-reads labels from fresh storage to avoid an ungroup-then-regroup flicker when the URL is genuinely a member, otherwise ungroups via pendingUngroups and clears the auto-group flag. Returns wait, kept, or ejected.
name: ejectAutoGroupedTab

description: Synthetic event factory: returns an event-shaped object with no-op stopPropagation/preventDefault spread with the given attributes, so code can feed a synthetic key into KeyDown.trigger (e.g. SearchResults triggering Escape after a click).
name: event

untestabilityReason.justification: synthetic event factory — object literal spread with no branching

description: Derives a deterministic favicon fallback monogram for a url row: the first visible letter (scheme/www. stripped, title preferred, '?' when empty) and a palette color chosen by hashing the url, so the same site always gets the same tile.
name: faviconMonogram

description: Formats the auto-close inactivity threshold in minutes for the Settings control, rendering Off for non-positive values, whole minutes under an hour, or hours with a single decimal only when not whole.
name: formatAutoClose

description: Pure fill math for the LoadMeter gauge, factored out of the SVG-mutation effect so it is testable without a DOM. Given a measured value, the arc base floor and max ceiling, returns the percent of segments that stay EMPTY (100 - filledFraction*100); a fuller gauge returns a smaller number. Intentionally un-clamped to mirror the reference.
name: gaugeFillPercent

description: Reads the current cursor drop-hover state from the external drag-hover store.
name: getDragHover

description: Promise/callback hybrid wrapper around chrome.storage.local.get: invokes the callback when supplied, otherwise resolves with the result.
name: getLocalStorage

description: Thin promise wrapper around chrome.system.cpu.getInfo() so pollSystemLoad can await it uniformly. Platform boundary to the Chrome system API.
name: getSystemCpuInfo

untestabilityReason.justification: Direct chrome.system.cpu API call; exercised indirectly through pollSystemLoad with a mocked chrome.system.

description: Thin promise wrapper around chrome.system.memory.getInfo() so pollSystemLoad can await it uniformly. Platform boundary to the Chrome system API.
name: getSystemMemoryInfo

untestabilityReason.justification: Direct chrome.system.memory API call; exercised indirectly through pollSystemLoad with a mocked chrome.system.

description: Promise wrapper around chrome.tabGroups.get that resolves null for absent/sentinel (-1) group ids.
name: getTabGroup

description: Derives a stable storage key for a URL by stripping the hash fragment and prefixing `url-`.
name: getUrlKey

description: Reconciles Chrome tab groups against the labels model: groups labeled tabs (creating/coloring groups as needed) and ungroups tabs that no longer belong to any label, skipping pinned tabs. Refuses to record a tab whose ungroup is in flight (in pendingUngroups) so a tab navigating out of a group is never captured into it.
name: groupTabs

description: Diffs old vs new activeTabs to reconcile label membership when a tab moves between groups, adding/removing its url key from the affected labels; seeds a missing destination label instead of throwing, skips tabs whose ungroup is pending, and no-ops without a previous value.
name: handleActiveTabsGroupChanges

description: Formats a numeric value with locale-aware thousands separators for the Load page's raw per-process table (1284 -> '1,284'). A falsy value yields undefined so the caller renders nothing; locale defaults to the document language then 'en', overridable via the lang arg. Factored out of the Load page so the formatting is unit-testable.
name: humanReadableNumber

description: Popup entry point: focuses the existing pinned TabCommand app tab if open, otherwise creates one at index 0, then closes the popup.
name: init

description: Channel-based degradation entry point for the Browser Load gauge, run at worker load. Uses chrome.processes when present (Dev/Canary, full per-tab fidelity, processProcesses tags loadDataSource 'processes'); else falls back to chrome.system.* polling ('system'); else writes loadDataSource 'none' so the UI can say there is no load data.
name: initLoadSource

description: Installs the in-app chrome shim on globalThis only when the real extension chrome.storage.local is absent (inert in a packaged extension, so production behavior is unchanged). Replaces the reference App.jsx's sinon-chrome fallback; called once at src/index.jsx before the first render. Returns true when it installed the shim, false when a real chrome was present.
name: installChromeShim

description: Predicate deciding whether an activeTabs entry may be auto-closed: not Chrome-pinned, not tabCommandPinned, not the active tab, and last active at or before the cutoff.
name: isAutoCloseEligible

description: Pure predicate deciding whether a URL is a real website worth recording into history/Favorites. Returns true only when the value is a non-empty string that parses as a URL whose protocol is exactly http: or https:; returns false for non-strings, empty/whitespace, unparseable values, and every other scheme such as about: including about:blank, file:, data:, view-source:, chrome:, chrome-extension:, devtools:, blob:. One rule replaces an ever-growing blocklist. Imported by the service worker's newUrl to gate what enters allUrls, and by rankFavorites to defensively drop already-stored non-website entries. Complements validTab which is tab-shaped and worker-only; this predicate is URL-shaped and stack-agnostic, mirroring the defensive try/new URL/catch style of normalizeUrl.
name: isTrackableUrl

description: Internal shim helper: resolves the callback argument from a variadic action-stub call by returning the last argument when it is a function, else undefined. Lets the no-op tabs/tabGroups stubs accept Chrome's varying (props, cb?) / (id, props, cb?) arities.
name: lastCallback

description: Subscribes processProcesses to chrome.processes.onUpdatedWithMemory, guarded by try/catch so a missing processes API (non-Chrome / no permission) degrades quietly.
name: listenToProcesses

description: Maps a load-bar width percentage to a CSS color variable (high >= 70, medium >= 40, otherwise light).
name: loadColor

description: Maps an overall browser-load percentage onto the three triage/gauge severity bands (low/medium/high) given the user warnAt threshold; the medium band begins at warnAt*0.6. Shared by the LoadMeter gauge and the Triage panel so both color the same load identically. Distinct from summarizeProcessLoad which yields a per-tab level.
name: loadLevel

description: Bidirectional Chrome group-color map: converts a named tab-group color to its hex value and vice versa. Lifted to module scope so both groupTabs and handleActiveTabsGroupChanges can color a seeded label.
name: mapColors

description: Internal helper of deriveSystemTotals: system memory pressure = (capacity - availableCapacity)/capacity, clamped 0..1. Returns 0 (never NaN) when the info object or capacity is missing.
name: memoryUtilization

description: Registers a newly-visited URL: prepends its url key to the capped (250) `allUrls` history, evicting the oldest non-labeled keys from storage, and returns the storage updates. No-ops without a tabId/url.
name: newUrl

description: Pure URL-normalization helper that produces a canonical grouping key for de-duplicating Favorites rows. Collapses cosmetic variants onto one key: drops the scheme so http and https are equivalent, lowercases the host and strips a leading www., strips a trailing slash from the path, and drops the fragment, while PRESERVING the query string and distinct paths. Defensive: a non-parseable URL falls back to the trimmed raw string so callers never throw. Used by rankFavorites to merge slash/www/protocol variants of the same site.
name: normalizeUrl

description: Extracts the integer Chrome tab id from a `tab-<n>` storage key.
name: parseTabId

description: Scales a value within a [base, max] window to a clamped 0-100 percentage for the triage load bands.
name: pct

description: One stable-Chrome sampling pass: reads chrome.system.cpu + chrome.system.memory, feeds the previous + current sample through deriveSystemTotals, and persists { processTotals, loadDataSource: 'system' }. On any thrown system call it degrades to loadDataSource 'none' and stops polling rather than crashing the worker.
name: pollSystemLoad

description: chrome.processes update handler (Dev/Canary): aggregates per-process CPU/network/memory totals and associates them with tabs, tags loadDataSource 'processes', persists the result, and after ~10 samples pauses listening for 15s to throttle.
name: processProcesses

description: Detects whether the Dev/Canary-only chrome.processes.onUpdatedWithMemory API is present, so the worker can choose the full-fidelity per-tab load path over the stable-Chrome system fallback.
name: processesApiAvailable

description: Removes auto-closed entries older than the retention window from the autoClosed map in place so the Automatically Closed list does not grow unbounded.
name: pruneAutoClosed

description: Service-worker mirror of the visitDecay pruneVisits helper: drops visit timestamps older than VISIT_RETENTION_MS (30 days), filters non-finite entries, sorts ascending, and caps to the newest MAX_VISITS (50). Applied on each newUrl write so per-site visit history recorded for Favorites time-decay ranking stays bounded. Duplicated here because the service-worker runtime cannot import the ES module, matching the GAUGE and AUTO_CLOSE constant-mirroring convention.
name: pruneVisits

description: Pure helper that bounds a per-site visit-timestamp array: it coerces entries to numbers, drops any older than the VISIT_RETENTION_MS horizon or non-finite, sorts the survivors ascending, and caps the result to the newest MAX_VISITS. Keeps stored visit history bounded on write in the service worker and normalizes visits before scoring in rankFavorites. Tolerates undefined or garbage input by returning an empty array. The service worker mirrors this logic because it cannot import the ES module.
name: pruneVisits

description: Pure ranking for the Favorites sidebar and View All page. SCORING is time-decayed: for each candidate it resolves visit timestamps via visitsFor (real visits, or a lazy seed from visitCount for legacy records), discounts a currently-open non-pinned tab by dropping its most-recent in-progress visit, de-duplicates candidates by normalizeUrl so http/https/www/trailing-slash variants collapse to one row with their visit timestamps merged, computes a decayedVisitScore over the merged visits, and drops any site scoring below QUALIFY_MIN. Survivors are ordered by decayed score descending, with latest-visit then list position as deterministic tiebreaks. OPEN DETECTION is page-identity aware: rather than an exact urlKey match, each candidate's isOpen is true when any live openKey shares its page identity (origin + pathname via samePageKey), so a favorite still lights the --open cue when a live tab has drifted only its query/hash (e.g. a Google-Doc ?tab= in-page rewrite) — matching how the tab-group eject path draws the in-page-rewrite vs navigation line. The normalizeUrl grouping/de-dupe key is left query-preserving, so query-distinct pages remain distinct rows even though their open cue follows page identity. Options: openKeys for the open-tab discount and page-identity open cue, hiddenKeys whose members are NOT dropped but returned flagged isHidden for the View All page, now for deterministic decay, plus halfLifeMs and qualifyMin overrides. excludedKeys removes matching urlKeys entirely; a limit of Infinity leaves the list uncapped. Each returned row carries urlKey, url, title, favicon, isOpen, isHidden, score, visitCount in window, lastVisit, and recentVisits for the sparkline. Non-website keys and title-less records are filtered out.
name: rankFavorites

description: Records a visit when a tab is activated (switched to), throttled per site to ACCESS_THROTTLE_MS. Resolves the activated tab, ignores untrackable/missing tabs, and only counts the access as a visit when the sites most recent visit is older than the throttle window, otherwise the open-then-activate sequence and alt-tabbing would double-count. Delegates the write to newUrl so access-visits and open-visits stay identical in shape.
name: recordAccess

description: Records an in-group tab's URL into its group's label, seeding the label with the group's mapped color when it does not exist yet, then persists via update. Runs only for non-auto-grouped tabs such as startup sync of pre-existing Chrome groups.
name: recordInGroupTab

description: Pure formatter that maps an epoch-ms timestamp to a compact how-long-ago label relative to a caller-supplied now: sub-hour reads as just now, under a day as N hours ago, under two weeks as N days ago, and beyond as N weeks ago. A missing timestamp reads as never, and a future timestamp is clamped to just now. Used by the Favorites View All page for each favorite's last-visited stat.
name: relativeTime

description: Returns a new labels map with a urlKey spliced out of the named label's urlKeys (fresh array, input not mutated; unknown label returns the map unchanged). Backs the UrlDetails Groups-chip removal. Extracted from the page's handleLabelClick.
name: removeUrlFromLabel

description: Attaches each label's resolved urls array (url/title/favicon, plus notes only when present) from the per-URL info map and drops urlKeys, returning the labels ready to JSON.stringify into the export.
name: resolveLabelUrls

description: Reduces a URL to its origin + pathname so the tab-grouping eject path can tell an in-page URL rewrite (query/hash change — keep grouped) apart from a real navigation (different origin/path — eject). Drops the query string and fragment; falls back to the raw input for non-string or unparseable values so they compare equal only when literally identical.
name: samePageKey

description: Computes the truncated, highlighted notes snippet under a URL search result whose match landed in its notes: finds the term case-insensitively, captures the original-cased match, windows ~18 chars before / ~24 after, and returns { before, match, after } (or null when there's nothing to highlight).
name: searchNotesSnippet

description: Pure migration helper that synthesizes a recent visit history for a legacy url record that has a visitCount but no visits array. It returns up to MAX_VISITS timestamps spread evenly across the last few days ending at now, so a pre-upgrade favorite keeps a sensible decayed rank on first load instead of vanishing, then converges to real timestamps as the user revisits. A zero or missing count yields an empty array; a count of one yields a single visit at now. This is a one-time, non-destructive approximation applied lazily at score time.
name: seedVisitsFromCount

description: Shapes raw minisearch hits for the SearchResults overlay: dedupes hits by document id (guarding the duplicate-React-key crash from overlapping index rebuilds), then splits them into { labels, urls } — a hit is a label when it carries labelTitle, otherwise a labeled URL.
name: segmentSearchResults

description: Updates the cursor drop-hover state and notifies subscribers, deduping identical updates so group cards only repaint when the hovered target changes. Kept outside React state so updating it mid-drag does not re-render the dragged tab's subtree and cancel the drag.
name: setDragHover

description: Sorts a labels map into the canonical export order: by title (locale compare) then by position, defaulting a missing position to 0. The two chained sorts mirror the reference ImportExport sortAndStuff.
name: sortLabels

description: Starts the stable-Chrome system-load poll loop: runs pollSystemLoad then re-schedules every SYSTEM_POLL_INTERVAL_MS, idempotently (guards against a double timer). Defensively switches back to the chrome.processes listener if that richer API appears mid-session.
name: startSystemLoadPolling

description: Event guard that stops propagation on UrlField key events, keeping the global keydown handler from hijacking typing inside the field. Reproduced faithfully from the original.
name: stopProp

untestabilityReason.justification: single-expression wrapper over event.stopPropagation() — no branching or logic to test

description: Clears the system-load poll timer and resets it to null, ending the stable-Chrome fallback sampling loop (used on switch-to-processes and on a system-call failure).
name: stopSystemLoadPolling

description: Subscribes a listener to cursor drop-hover changes and returns an unsubscribe function, letting each group card repaint independently as the cursor moves between groups.
name: subscribeDragHover

description: Summarizes a stored per-URL processes record into the load readout a tab shows: average CPU and private-memory, a severity level (low/medium/high/excessive), and a bar width. Returns null when there is no sampled data so callers render nothing. Shared by the Url tab rows and the Load page LoadUrl cards (extracted from the inline copy that lived in Url).
name: summarizeProcessLoad

description: Detects whether chrome.system.cpu and chrome.system.memory are both present, gating the stable-Chrome whole-browser load fallback that drives the gauge when chrome.processes is unavailable.
name: systemApiAvailable

description: Builds the per-url update record for a tab — resolving an empty object for invalid tabs and otherwise merging the stored url with fresh tab/process metadata via urlUpdates.
name: tabUpdates

description: Records a Chrome tab group's title keyed by its integer id in the worker's in-memory `groups` map, used to resolve a tab's label when its group changes.
name: trackGroup

description: Thin write helper that persists an updates object to chrome.storage.local.set; the single storage-write chokepoint for the worker.
name: update

description: Queries all normal-window tabs and rebuilds the persisted `activeTabs` list (urlKey, pinned, groupId, timestamps, auto-closed state), debounced via waitingToUpdate, then writes it to chrome.storage.local.
name: updateActiveTabs

description: Accumulates a single process's cpu/network/privateMemory/jsMemoryAllocated/jsMemoryUsed into the running processTotals, treating missing metrics as 0.
name: updateTotals

description: Predicate for whether a urlKey is a deliberate, recorded member of a tab-group label. Centralizes the membership check shared by the auto-group ejection and recording paths; tolerates a missing label by returning false.
name: urlKeyIsMember

description: Merges a tab's current title/favicon/groupId into a stored url record and accumulates rolling process metrics, normalizing the running averages once a sample window exceeds 100.
name: urlUpdates

description: Predicate used by rankFavorites: true when a url record exists and has a non-empty string title, so favorite candidates without a renderable title are filtered out of the ranking.
name: usableTitle

description: Pure helper returning the largest single-bucket visit count across every favorite's daily AND weekly usage buckets, floored at 1. The Favorites View All page passes this as a common maximum to every row's UsageSparkline so bar heights are comparable across rows — a busy site reads visibly taller than a quiet one — instead of each chart self-scaling to its own busiest bucket. Uses bucketVisitsByDay and bucketVisitsByWeek.
name: usageMax

description: React hook owning the resolved light/dark theme. Resolves from three inputs: the saved themePreference storage key ['system' | 'light' | 'dark', default system], the live OS prefers-color-scheme tracked via matchMedia [dark fallback], and an in-memory temporary override set by the sun/moon toggle. Only the resolved value mirrors to the document element's data-theme attribute, which the CSS token layer keys off so the whole UI re-themes from one place. In system mode an OS day/night transition clears the override; a legacy theme:'light' migrates to a Day preference. Returns [theme, toggleTheme].
name: useTheme

description: Predicate that excludes empty and browser-internal tabs (chrome://, devtools://, chrome-extension://) from tracking.
name: validTab

description: Private helper in rankFavorites that resolves a url record's effective visit timestamps for scoring: it returns the record's own pruned visits when present, otherwise lazily seeds them from visitCount via seedVisitsFromCount so a legacy record still ranks, and returns an empty array when neither is available. Always returns a retention-bounded, length-capped ascending array via pruneVisits.
name: visitsFor
